channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising: Release Date, Pre-Order Guide & Card List
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon TCG continues to expand with new sets, and the release date for Chaos Rising has been revealed, and the pre-order period is likely around the corner.

Chaos Rising continues Pokémon TCG’s focus on Mega Evolution Pokémon, with previous sets having featured the likes of Mega Charizard, Mega Lucario, Mega Venusaur, and Mega Zygarde.

Now, it’s time for Mega Greninja to take center stage, and if you want all the important details on the set’s release, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When Does Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Release?

The Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising expansion is scheduled for a worldwide release on May 22, 2026, with a pre-release weekend occurring earlier in the month.

Chaos Rising is the third Pokémon TCG set in 2026, following on from Ascended Heroes and Perfect Order, continuing the trend of showcasing Mega Evolution Pokémon that first appeared in Legends: Z-A.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokémon in Pokopia & How to Get Them

The prelease event for Chaos Rising takes place on May 9, 2026, two weeks ahead of the release, at participating Pokémon Play! Stores.

While the worldwide release covers major regions, including the US, Europe, and Australia, the Japanese versions of the set have separate release dates, while China works off a unique release process.

When Are Chaos Rising Pre-Orders Available?

Pre-order dates for Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising are yet to be confirmed, and The Pokémon Company never announces in advance when the drop on the Pokémon Center Online takes place.

Securing product has become a difficult challenge for many players, with the Pokémon Center having a queue in place that limits access. Due to the high demand, no announcements are made on exactly when pre-orders go live.

However, there has been a trend in the past year or so, with pre-orders at the Pokémon Center for a new set opening just before the release of the prior set.

If that trend continues to be the case, Pokémon Center pre-orders for Chaos Rising should take place in the next few weeks ahead of the release of Perfect Order on March 27.

Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Card List

The full card list for Chaos Rising has not been revealed, though it will primarily be comprised of cards from Japan’s Ninja Spinner and Nihil Zero sets.

Standout cards include the Mega Greninja SIR, Froakie Illustration Rare, and Rowlet Illustration Rare. However, some cards from the Japanese sets will be pulled to become promos, including those chosen for the Elite Trainer Box.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Pokopia Pokémon Habitats & How to Make Them

As outlined by the press release announcing Chaos Rising, the set will contain the following chase cards:

  • Five Mega Evolution Pokémon ex
  • Five Pokémon ex
  • 11 illustration rare Pokémon
  • 18 ultra rare Pokémon and Trainer cards 
  • Six special illustration rare Pokémon and Supporter cards

Are you excited for the release of Chaos Rising in the Pokémon TCG? Share your thoughts on the new set in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, find out what happened to the humans in Pokopia, and read about Pokopia’s impressive sales numbers.

MOBILE APP

item.title: PEGI Says All Loot Box Titles to Be Rated PEGI 16
item.content:encoded:
A new change to age ratings in Europe will mean games with loot boxes are rated PEGI 16.

Loot boxes have always been a topic of contention in the gaming industry, and it seems a major change is coming to this space soon. We recently saw Valve being hit by a class-action lawsuit accusing it of running an illegal gambling operation. Now, PEGI is also changing ratings for games with micro-transactions and loot box systems.

PEGI Ratings Changes Will Arrive in June 2026

There are four categories under which we will see changes to PEGI (Pan-European Game Information) age ratings. The first up are in-app purchases, where games that include paid battle passes, time-limited items that would influence people to return to the game, will receive a PEGI 12 rating. Games that feature NFTs or blockchain-related mechanisms will receive a PEGI 18 rating.

Regarding loot boxes, games with paid random items will receive a PEGI 16 rating and, in some cases, a PEGI 18 rating; games like the EA Sports FC series could be most affected, as they are currently rated PEGI 3. Games with systems that reward players for returning to the game, such as daily quests (play-by-appointment), will receive a PEGI 7 rating, and if those systems punish players for not returning, such as by losing content or reducing progress, they will receive a PEGI 12 rating.

Lastly, if a game has no restrictions on communication features, such as blocking and reporting, it will receive a PEGI 18 rating. Here’s what Dirk Bosmans, Director of PEGI, stated on the new changes:

“It was incredibly useful to learn from the experiences of our colleagues in Germany. We are confident that these ambitious updates to PEGI’s classification criteria will provide parents and players with more useful and transparent advice that better reflects the overall experience that players can expect from the video games they play.”

In other news, dataminers have uncovered strings suggesting that controversial loot boxes could be coming to Marvel Rivals. Additionally, here’s Valve’s statement responding to New York’s lawsuit, disputing claims its loot boxes are illegal gambling. What are your thoughts on the PEGI age ratings changes? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Pokémon Pokopia surpassing 2.2 million global sales in 4 days. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: League of Legends Major Arena Update and Season 2 Theme Leak
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends is preparing a huge Arena update and a Demon theme for Season 2 of 2026, according to what appears to be an accidental leak from Gamescom.

Riot Games has been confirmed to be attending the Gamescom LATAM event in Brazil, and an Instagram post announcing this has also given LoL fans an early glimpse into what is coming up for the MOBA.

The official post from Gamescom LATAM, when translated, says: “And of course, League of Legends will also be present with the major update to Arena mode, bringing new maps, systems, and skins inspired by the demons of Runeterra.”

The skin release of each season typically revolves around its lore theme, seemingly confirming next season’s focus. There may still be a bit of a wait for this update though, as Season 2 didn’t start until April 30th last year.

Like last year, Riot is pushing three themed seasons in League of Legends this year, which will change the cosmetics of Summoner’s Rift and affect the lore in an ongoing story that will run throughout the year.

So far, 2026 has seen a Demacia theme headlined by the upcoming Shyvana VGU rework and the city-building minigame Demacia Rising in the LoL client.

Demons is an intriguing theme for a season, and fan theories are already running wild on what it might entail.

The League of Legends wiki has four champions labelled as demons: Evelynn, Fiddlesticks, Nocturne, and Tahm Kench.

After the ‘Salvation’ cinematic earlier this year showed Sona fighting off shadowy creatures in a forest, Nocturne feels a safe bet for one of the stars of next season.

It has also been confirmed that we’re getting a new LoL champion in Season 2. Perhaps they’ll be a demon or rather someone who battles them.

The Arena game mode is apparently getting a major rehaul soon, and it’s probably needed.

Arena has fallen in popularity after the huge success of ARAM Mayhem, which essentially added Arena augments into the ‘All Random All Mid’ mode.

ARAM Mayhem was supposed to leave LoL at the end of 2025, but it seems to have become a permanent fixture after the LoL fan base craved more.

This seeming leak tells us that Arena is seemingly getting new maps and gameplay systems to refresh the mode. Arena hasn’t received a new map since March last year, when the Reckoner Arena was added with destructible pillars you can knock over onto opponents.

After Mayhem received Teamfight Tactics- style Augment sets, which reward players with bonus abilities for collecting multiple augments from within a set.

What changes do you want to see to Arena in LoL? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And check out what other changes Riot are making with the LoL 26.6 patch notes preview.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Top 20 PC Games of 2025 Aren’t Hugely Surprising
item.content:encoded:
NewZoo has published a fresh report about the state of the gaming industry through 2025 and beyond. It’s a massive, 80-page beast that covers every angle of the industry possible, including what games are occupying the top spots across multiple platforms.

One slide refers to the top twenty games on PC through 2025, ranked by playtime. It’s not a hugely surprising mix, but there are a couple of outliers that broke into the ranking for the first time.

Battle Royale Falls, Free to Play Remains King

At the top of the PC table sit the free-to-play titles that have remained in those lofty spots for years. The number-one spot is owned by Roblox, with 9.7% of all PC playtime through 2025. It’s followed closely behind by Counter-Strike 2, which boasts a handsome 7% market share, and then League of Legends, just nipped by CS2 with a 6.9% share.

The data reveals that battle royale games are plummeting out of favor with gaming fans, as Apex Legends barely managed to claim a spot in the top rankings with a 0.9% market share. It’s joined in that spot by Genshin Impact and Wuthering Waves, beaten ever so slightly by Diablo IV with a 1% share.

What’s surprising is the emergence of Escape from Tarkov in 11th place, nestled between Call of Duty and Overwatch 2. The extraction shooter from Battlestate Games managed to beat PUBG, GTA 5, and Marvel Rivals in 2025 in the playtime stakes, securing a 1.7% share.

But it’s important to point out that it came from early access and had millions of players backing it from a decade on the market, so there’s that.

PC’s success is coming from a ‘rapidly expanding long tail’, which is how NewZoo described the growth. While console-based players typically stick to the top twenty games, PC goes beyond that, with the majority of playtime being made up from the backlog of tens of thousands of PC games out there.

So, there’s the data from NewZoo. These evergreen titles like Roblox and Counter-Strike remain king, while battle royale games are collapsing, and extraction shooters continue to be disruptive.

Are you surprised at all about this ranking? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the latest Counter-Strike 2 update

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
